events.text: The patient is a 48-year-old female named Margaret Okonkwo. She is of Nigerian descent, born in Lagos and residing in the United States for 20 years. She works as a licensed practical nurse. She has a history of hypertension managed with lisinopril, type 2 diabetes mellitus managed with metformin, and a prior diagnosis of myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) with refractory cytopenia with multilineage dysplasia (RCMD) identified 18 months prior, for which she was observed without treatment. She is a never-smoker and consumes alcohol rarely. She has no known drug allergies.

At age 48 years, the patient had a diagnosis of AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), per 2022 WHO classification, corresponding to Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML) (SEER site code 9861/3), arising from antecedent MDS. Presenting AJCC/pathologic stage: not applicable (leukemia). Peripheral blood WBC at diagnosis: 84,000/uL (leukocytosis). Hemoglobin: 7.1 g/dL (severe anemia). Platelets: 28,000/uL (severe thrombocytopenia). Presenting symptoms included fatigue, dyspnea on exertion, gingival bleeding, easy bruising, and 2-week history of low-grade fevers. Bone marrow biopsy confirmed 62% myeloblasts. Cytogenetics showed normal female karyotype (46,XX) in 18/20 metaphases with no complex abnormalities. FLT3-ITD mutation detected with high allelic ratio (VAF 0.82, ITD insert length 39 bp). Auer rods present on peripheral smear. LDH elevated at 890 U/L. Confirmation: POSITIVE HISTOLOGY; Primary site: BONE MARROW (C421.9); Histology: Acute myeloid leukemia, NOS (9861/3).

At age 48 years, the patient had a bone marrow biopsy and aspirate performed at diagnosis. Biopsy showed a hypercellular marrow (95% cellularity) with 62% CD34-positive myeloblasts by flow cytometry immunophenotyping. Blasts expressed CD33+, CD117+, CD13+, HLA-DR+, CD34+, CD123+, partial CD7+. Myeloperoxidase (MPO) positive in a subset. Erythroid and megakaryocytic lineages showed dysplastic morphology consistent with underlying MDS. Reticulin fibrosis: MF-1. Immunohistochemistry: CD34 highlights blasts, MPO positive. Cytogenetics (karyotype): 46,XX [18/20], with 2 metaphases showing del(7q) (residual MDS-related clone). FISH panel: FLT3-ITD positive (high allelic ratio). NPM1: wild-type. CEBPA: wild-type. IDH1/IDH2: negative. TP53: wild-type by sequencing. Pathologic diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related changes.

At age 48 years, the patient had next-generation sequencing (NGS) performed for acute myeloid leukemia based on a specimen obtained from bone marrow aspirate at diagnosis. A 500-gene hematologic malignancy panel was used. Actionable alterations: FLT3 p.ITD (internal tandem duplication, exon 14-15 junction, allelic ratio 0.82, VAF 82%) - HIGH ALLELIC RATIO, targetable with midostaurin/gilteritinib. Additional somatic mutations: DNMT3A p.R882H (VAF 44%) - epigenetic regulator, poor prognostic association; SRSF2 p.P95H (VAF 38%) - splicing factor mutation, associated with MDS-related changes; ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12 (VAF 41%) - chromatin remodeling, associated with MDS/AML-MRC; SF3B1: wild-type; TET2: wild-type; NPM1: wild-type; CEBPA: wild-type; IDH1: wild-type; IDH2: wild-type; RUNX1 p.R204Q (VAF 35%) - transcription factor, associated with MDS-related AML; WT1: wild-type; NRAS: wild-type; KRAS: wild-type; KIT: wild-type; TP53: wild-type; PTPN11: wild-type; CBL: wild-type; EZH2: wild-type; STAG2: wild-type; RAD21: wild-type; SMC1A: wild-type; PHF6: wild-type; BCOR: wild-type; BCORL1: wild-type; KDM6A: wild-type. Copy number alterations: del(7q) detected (partial, low clone). Fusions: none detected (FLT3-ITD confirmed, not a fusion). Microsatellite instability: stable (MSS). Tumor mutational burden: 4.2 mutations/Mb (low). Risk stratification per ELN 2022: ADVERSE (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio + DNMT3A + ASXL1 + SRSF2 + RUNX1 co-mutations; AML-MRC features). Recommendation: allogeneic stem cell transplant in first remission if CR achieved.

At age 48 years, the patient had a CT scan of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast performed at staging workup prior to induction chemotherapy. Findings: Mild splenomegaly (spleen 14.2 cm craniocaudal). No hepatomegaly. No lymphadenopathy (mediastinal, hilar, or abdominal). No pleural effusions. No pericardial effusion. Lungs: clear bilaterally without consolidation or masses. No lytic or blastic bony lesions. No soft tissue masses or organomegaly suggesting extramedullary AML (chloroma). Impression: Mild splenomegaly consistent with known leukemia. No evidence of extramedullary disease. Radiology assessment: cancer present (bone marrow disease, leukemia).

At age 48 years, prior to induction chemotherapy, the patient had a 2D echocardiogram performed for cardiac clearance. Findings: Normal left ventricular size and systolic function. Ejection fraction 62%. No wall motion abnormalities. Normal diastolic function. No valvular disease of hemodynamic significance. No pericardial effusion. Impression: Normal cardiac function. Cleared for anthracycline-based chemotherapy.

At age 48 years, the patient was admitted to the inpatient hematology unit and initiated on curative-intent 7+3 induction chemotherapy plus midostaurin for AML with FLT3-ITD (high allelic ratio), AML-MRC, ELN adverse risk. Regimen: Cytarabine 200 mg/m2/day continuous IV infusion on days 1-7 (total dose per cycle: ~2800 mg/m2); Daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 IV push on days 1, 2, and 3; Midostaurin 50 mg orally twice daily on days 8-21 (FLT3 inhibitor added per RATIFY trial protocol). Supportive care: allopurinol for tumor lysis prophylaxis (held after day 3 as WBC responded); antimicrobial prophylaxis with fluconazole, acyclovir, and levofloxacin; aggressive IV hydration; anti-emetics (ondansetron, lorazepam); blood product transfusion support (pRBCs and platelets as needed). Treatment intent: curative; bridge to allogeneic stem cell transplant in CR1 given adverse-risk disease.

At age 48 years (approximately day 10 of induction), the patient developed febrile neutropenia (temperature 38.9 degrees C, ANC < 100/uL). Blood cultures drawn. Empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics initiated with cefepime 2g IV q8h. Subsequently, blood cultures grew Escherichia coli (1/2 bottles) sensitive to cefepime. Antibiotics continued for 14 days with resolution of fever by day 5 of antibiotic therapy. Additional adverse events during induction: Grade 3 mucositis (WHO grade 3) requiring morphine PCA for pain control and TPN for 8 days; Grade 2 nausea and vomiting managed with combination antiemetics; Grade 3 thrombocytopenia requiring platelet transfusions (2-3 times per week); Grade 3 anemia requiring pRBC transfusions (Hgb maintained above 8 g/dL); Grade 1 transaminase elevation (ALT peak 78 U/L, self-limited). No midostaurin dose reduction required. No cardiac toxicity.

At age 48 years (day 21 of induction, count nadir assessment), the patient had an inpatient oncology assessment. She remained hospitalized with persistent neutropenia (ANC 0/uL). Mucositis improving. Fever resolved. Tolerating oral intake. Repeat peripheral blood showed no circulating blasts. Bone marrow biopsy deferred to day 28-30 per protocol given expected hypoplasia. Plan: continue supportive care, await count recovery. Midostaurin continued through day 21. Overall clinical trajectory: anticipated aplasia, no concerning features for primary refractory disease at this time.

At age 48 years (day 30 post-induction, bone marrow re-staging biopsy), the patient had a bone marrow biopsy and aspirate performed. WBC had recovered to 1,800/uL, ANC 1,200/uL. Biopsy findings: Hypocellular marrow (25% cellularity) with regenerative trilineage hematopoiesis. Myeloblasts by morphology: less than 2%. No Auer rods identified. Flow cytometry: No aberrant CD34+ blast population detected; blasts less than 1% of total cellularity. Cytogenetics: 46,XX [20/20] - no del(7q) clone detected. Morphologic complete remission (CR) confirmed per ELN 2022 criteria. MRD assessment by 8-color flow cytometry: NEGATIVE (sensitivity threshold 0.01%; no leukemia-associated immunophenotype detected). FLT3-ITD by PCR: not detected (below assay sensitivity). Pathologic diagnosis: Complete remission with MRD negativity.

At age 48 years (approximately 5 weeks after induction start), the patient had an outpatient oncology clinic visit following discharge from inpatient hospitalization. She was discharged home 3 days after confirming CR. CBC: WBC 3,200/uL, Hgb 10.8 g/dL, platelets 142,000/uL - counts recovering well. No active bleeding or infection. Performance status ECOG 1. Midostaurin 50 mg BID continued as maintenance during consolidation planning phase. Discussion: Given ELN adverse-risk disease (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A/ASXL1/SRSF2/RUNX1 co-mutations, AML-MRC), allogeneic stem cell transplant (alloSCT) in CR1 is strongly recommended per current guidelines. HLA typing initiated. Referred to bone marrow transplant (BMT) team. Sibling HLA typing: no matched sibling identified. Unrelated donor search initiated via National Marrow Donor Program (NMDP/Be The Match). Cancer present: NO. Responding: YES (MRD-negative CR1).

At age 48 years (approximately 6 weeks post-induction start), the patient was admitted for consolidation chemotherapy cycle 1 while awaiting unrelated donor identification and transplant logistics. Regimen: High-dose cytarabine (HiDAC) 3 g/m2 IV over 3 hours every 12 hours on days 1, 3, and 5 (6 doses total per cycle). Midostaurin 50 mg PO BID on days 8-21 continued per RATIFY protocol. Supportive medications: steroid eye drops (prednisolone acetate) for cytarabine-related conjunctivitis prophylaxis; prophylactic antimicrobials continued. Treatment intent: maintain remission and reduce MRD burden while awaiting transplant. One cycle planned prior to transplant given availability of 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor identified within 8 weeks.

At age 48 years (during HiDAC consolidation cycle 1), the patient developed Grade 2 cerebellar toxicity (ataxia, dysmetria) on day 6 of high-dose cytarabine, attributed to cytarabine neurotoxicity. Cytarabine held after 5 of 6 planned doses. Neurology consulted. Symptoms improved over 5 days with supportive care. No lasting neurologic deficit at time of discharge. Grade 3 neutropenia: ANC nadir 0/uL (day 14). No febrile neutropenia this cycle. Grade 2 mucositis: managed conservatively. Count recovery: ANC >1000/uL by day 28. No additional consolidation cycles administered; patient proceeded directly to pre-transplant evaluation given donor availability and acceptable toxicity profile.

At age 48 years (pre-transplant restaging, approximately 3.5 months after AML diagnosis), the patient had a PET-CT scan performed as pre-transplant restaging. Findings: No focal hypermetabolic lesions in lymph nodes, liver, spleen, or extramedullary sites. Mild residual splenomegaly (12.1 cm, improved from 14.2 cm at diagnosis). No evidence of chloroma or extramedullary AML. Bone marrow: mild heterogeneous FDG uptake pattern consistent with regenerating marrow post-chemotherapy. No abnormal skeletal lesions. Impression: No evidence of metabolically active disease. Compatible with complete remission. Radiology cancer status: no cancer identified on imaging.

At age 48 years (pre-transplant bone marrow biopsy, approximately 3.5 months after diagnosis), the patient had a repeat bone marrow biopsy performed at pre-transplant evaluation. Biopsy findings: Normocellular marrow (45% cellularity) with normal trilineage hematopoiesis. Myeloblasts less than 2% by morphology. Flow cytometry: No leukemia-associated immunophenotype detected (MRD NEGATIVE, sensitivity 0.01%). Cytogenetics: 46,XX [20/20]. FLT3-ITD by digital PCR: not detected (sensitivity < 0.01%). DNMT3A p.R882H by ddPCR: not detected. Conclusion: Sustained MRD-negative complete remission confirmed. Patient cleared for allogeneic stem cell transplant.

At age 48 years (approximately 4 months after AML diagnosis), the patient underwent matched unrelated donor (MUD) allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (alloHSCT). Donor: 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated male donor (A, B, C, DRB1, DQB1 allele-level match), CMV seronegative donor to CMV seropositive recipient (D-/R+ mismatch). Stem cell source: peripheral blood stem cells (PBSC), CD34+ cell dose 5.8 x 10^6/kg. Conditioning regimen: Reduced-intensity conditioning (RIC) with Fludarabine 30 mg/m2/day IV on days -6 to -2 (5 days) plus Busulfan 3.2 mg/kg/day IV on days -3 and -2 (2 days, IV busulfan with pharmacokinetic targeting, AUC 5,000 uM*min/day). GVHD prophylaxis: Tacrolimus (target trough 8-12 ng/mL) plus Methotrexate 15 mg/m2 on day +1, then 10 mg/m2 on days +3, +6, and +11. ATG (rabbit anti-thymocyte globulin, Thymoglobulin) 2.5 mg/kg/day IV on days -3, -2, -1. Midostaurin discontinued prior to conditioning. Transplant day 0: stem cells infused without acute infusion reaction. Supportive care: CMV surveillance by weekly PCR post-engraftment; PCP prophylaxis with trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (or dapsone); antifungal prophylaxis with voriconazole; acyclovir for HSV/VZV prophylaxis; G-CSF held per institutional protocol post-PBSC transplant.

At age 48 years (day +18 post-alloHSCT), the patient had an inpatient transplant team assessment. Neutrophil engraftment confirmed (ANC >500/uL for 3 consecutive days, first noted day +16). Platelet engraftment: platelets >20,000/uL unsupported day +20. Overall engraftment: successful. No acute infusion reactions. No veno-occlusive disease/sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (VOD/SOS): bilirubin and transaminases within normal limits, no hepatomegaly or weight gain exceeding 5%. No acute GVHD skin rash, GI symptoms, or liver enzyme elevation meeting GVHD criteria (Grade 0 at this time). CMV PCR: negative. Patient tolerating oral tacrolimus and prophylactic medications. Performance status improving. Chimerism testing sent. Discharge planned. Cancer present: NO.

At age 48 years (day +30 post-alloHSCT, chimerism and MRD assessment), the patient had bone marrow biopsy and chimerism testing performed. Bone marrow biopsy: Normocellular marrow (50% cellularity), normal trilineage hematopoiesis, no morphologic evidence of AML relapse (blasts < 2%). Flow cytometry MRD: NEGATIVE. Donor chimerism (whole marrow): 98% donor by STR analysis. T-cell chimerism: 95% donor. Myeloid chimerism: 99% donor. FLT3-ITD by digital PCR: not detected. DNMT3A p.R882H by ddPCR: not detected. Impression: Successful full donor engraftment with MRD-negative complete remission confirmed at day +30. No evidence of graft failure or relapse.

At age 48 years (day +45 post-alloHSCT), the patient was found to have CMV reactivation. CMV PCR: 1,840 IU/mL (above institutional threshold of 250 IU/mL for pre-emptive treatment). Patient was asymptomatic (no CMV end-organ disease). Pre-emptive therapy initiated with Valganciclovir 900 mg PO BID. Serial weekly CMV PCR monitoring performed. CMV PCR became undetectable by day +60. Valganciclovir stepped down to prophylactic dosing (900 mg daily) and continued through day +100. No CMV end-organ disease developed. Tacrolimus levels stable; no dose adjustment required for CMV management.

At age 48 years (approximately day +90 post-alloHSCT), the patient developed signs and symptoms of mild chronic graft-versus-host disease (cGVHD). Manifestations: (1) Skin: lichenoid changes on the forearms and dorsal hands, mild pruritis, no ulceration or sclerosis (skin involvement ~15% BSA by modified Rodnan score); (2) Mouth: mild xerostomia and lichenoid changes of buccal mucosa, no ulcers, mild pain score 2/10; (3) Eyes: mild dry eye symptoms, Schirmer's test 7 mm (borderline). No liver, lung, GI, or musculoskeletal cGVHD. NIH Global Severity Score: Mild (1 organ with score 1, overall score 1). Tacrolimus taper held; dose maintained at therapeutic level. Topical clobetasol cream initiated for skin lesions. Oral dexamethasone rinse initiated for oral mucosal lesions. Ophthalmology referred; artificial tears and cyclosporine eye drops initiated. No systemic steroids required at this time. FEV1/FVC normal (no bronchiolitis obliterans).

At age 48 years (day +100 post-alloHSCT, 100-day evaluation), the patient had a formal 100-day transplant assessment. Overall status: excellent. Performance status ECOG 1. Chronic GVHD: mild, stable on topical therapies - skin lichenoid changes improving with topical clobetasol; oral mucosa improving with dexamethasone rinse; dry eyes managed with cyclosporine eye drops. No systemic immunosuppression beyond tacrolimus. CBC: WBC 4,800/uL (ANC 3,100/uL), Hgb 11.9 g/dL, platelets 168,000/uL. BMP: normal. Tacrolimus trough: 8.2 ng/mL. Repeat bone marrow biopsy deferred to day +180 per protocol given excellent clinical status. CMV PCR: negative (off valganciclovir prophylaxis as of day +100). PCP prophylaxis continued. Cancer present: NO. Remission status: MRD-negative CR1 maintained.

At age 48 years (day +180 post-alloHSCT, 6-month restaging), the patient had a CT scan of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast performed as 6-month post-transplant restaging. Findings: Normal spleen size (9.8 cm craniocaudal, resolved prior splenomegaly). No hepatomegaly. No lymphadenopathy. No pleural effusions. Lungs: clear bilaterally, no consolidation, no ground-glass opacities (no bronchiolitis obliterans pattern). No evidence of fungal infection or opportunistic pneumonia. No lytic or blastic bony lesions. No extramedullary disease. Impression: No evidence of AML relapse or extramedullary disease. Resolved splenomegaly. No pulmonary complications from transplant or GVHD. Radiology cancer status: no cancer identified.

At age 48 years (day +180 post-alloHSCT, 6-month bone marrow biopsy), the patient had a protocol bone marrow biopsy and aspirate. Biopsy findings: Normocellular marrow (40% cellularity), normal trilineage hematopoiesis, no morphologic evidence of AML. Myeloblasts less than 2% by morphology and flow cytometry. MRD by 8-color flow cytometry: NEGATIVE (sensitivity 0.01%). Donor chimerism: 99% donor (whole marrow), 98% donor (T-cells), 99% donor (myeloid). FLT3-ITD digital PCR: not detected. DNMT3A p.R882H ddPCR: not detected. ASXL1 frameshift ddPCR: not detected. Conclusion: Durable MRD-negative complete remission with full donor chimerism maintained at 6 months post-alloHSCT. Excellent molecular clearance of all tracked mutations.

At age 49 years (approximately 12 months post-alloHSCT, one-year transplant anniversary evaluation), the patient had a comprehensive annual transplant evaluation. Overall status: very good. Performance status ECOG 1. Chronic GVHD: mild, ongoing but improving - skin lichenoid changes nearly resolved (< 5% BSA); oral GVHD in remission (no active lesions, mild xerostomia persisting); dry eyes stable with eye drops. Tacrolimus successfully tapered to 1 mg daily (near discontinuation). No systemic steroids. No new organ involvement. CBC: WBC 5,600/uL, Hgb 12.8 g/dL, platelets 210,000/uL. BMP: normal. HbA1c: 6.8% (type 2 DM well-controlled on metformin resumed). Pulmonary function tests: FEV1 88% predicted, DLCO 82% predicted (mildly reduced DLCO, consistent with prior chemotherapy and/or GVHD, no obstructive pattern). Bone marrow biopsy: MRD negative, full donor chimerism (99% donor). Patient returned to part-time nursing work. Discussions: immunosuppression taper to completion planned over next 3 months; continued surveillance per transplant protocol. Vaccinations: inactivated vaccines resumed per post-transplant vaccination schedule. Cancer present: NO. Disease status: MRD-negative CR1, sustained 12 months post-alloHSCT.

At age 49 years (18 months post-alloHSCT), the patient had a CT scan of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast as surveillance imaging. Findings: No evidence of disease relapse. Normal organ sizes. No lymphadenopathy. No masses or lesions concerning for extramedullary AML. Lungs: clear. No pleural or pericardial effusion. Impression: No evidence of AML recurrence. Ongoing complete remission. Radiology cancer status: no cancer identified.

At age 49 years (18 months post-alloHSCT), the patient had a routine outpatient oncology/BMT follow-up visit. She had successfully completed tacrolimus taper and was fully off all immunosuppression as of month 15 post-transplant. Chronic GVHD: resolved by NIH criteria (no active manifestations requiring treatment; mild residual xerostomia managed with saliva substitutes and pilocarpine 5 mg TID). CBC: WBC 6,200/uL, Hgb 13.4 g/dL, platelets 234,000/uL - fully recovered counts. Peripheral NK cell and T-cell recovery confirmed by flow cytometry lymphocyte subset panel (CD4 count 380/uL, recovering appropriately). Metabolic panel: normal hepatic and renal function. Patient resumed full-time work. Bone marrow biopsy performed confirming ongoing MRD-negative remission with 99% donor chimerism. No signs of late effects (no avascular necrosis, no cataracts, no secondary malignancy on surveillance). Quality of life assessment (FACT-BMT): substantially improved compared to 6 months post-transplant. Next follow-up: 3 months (24-month evaluation). Cancer present: NO. Disease status: sustained MRD-negative CR1, 18 months post-alloHSCT, off all immunosuppression.

scenario_blurb: Acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with FLT3-ITD mutation (high allelic ratio) in a 48-year-old, presenting with leukocytosis, anemia, and gingival bleeding. Treated with 7+3 induction (cytarabine/daunorubicin) plus midostaurin, achieves CR after first induction. MRD negative by flow cytometry. Proceeds to matched unrelated donor allogeneic stem cell transplant with mild chronic GVHD.
